canonical_definition: The property of certain weight matrices (particularly delta weights) where most of the information is concentrated in a small number of singular vectors, enabling efficient approximation via low-rank decomposition.
papers: Delta Decompression for MoE-based LLMs Compression
From Low Rank Gradient Subspace Stabilization to Low-Rank Weights: Observations, Theories, and Applications
